dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, we lost people in the last attack. Good people. And the ones who survived are falling apart.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The {enemy_type} raid on {location} took three lives: a guard, a farmer, and an elder who refused to abandon the storehouse. We drove them off, but the victory feels hollow. {target_npc} hasn't spoken since the burial, and half the settlement is talking about abandoning this place entirely. I need someone to gather {quest_item} from the old memorial grounds {location_hint} so we can build a proper remembrance. The {enemy_type} are still prowling the roads, but if we don't give people something to rally around, grief will destroy what the raid couldn't.
dialogueChunks.outro: We need to mourn before we can rebuild. And we need help doing both.

playerResponses.text: Honoring the dead gives the living a reason to keep going. I'll help.
Memorial materials through hostile territory. It's somber work, but I'll do it.
Grief counseling isn't in my skill set. I'm sorry for your loss.

dialogueChunks.intro: My husband was killed on the road two weeks ago, {player_name}. I've made my peace with that. But there's one thing I can't let go of.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: He was carrying {quest_item} when the {enemy_type} ambushed his caravan {location_hint}. It was a gift he'd made for our daughter's naming day: the only thing of his she'd have to remember him by. The caravan was looted and the bodies were brought back, but {quest_item} was gone. {target_npc} says the {enemy_type} probably took it to their camp along with the other spoils. I know it's just an object. But it's the last piece of him that hasn't been buried.
dialogueChunks.outro: I'm not asking you to avenge him. I'm asking you to bring home the one thing my daughter will hold when she asks about her father.

playerResponses.text: A father's last gift to his daughter. I'll find it, no matter what's in the way.
Raiding an enemy camp for a keepsake. It's personal, but I'll do it for a price.
Charging into an enemy camp for a single item is too dangerous.

bindings.quest_stakes: meaning in the wake of loss

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, I should have died instead. I know that's not a productive thought, but it's the only one I have most days.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: My partner and I were building something together: a watchtower at the edge of {location} to protect the settlement. They were gathering {quest_item} from {location_hint} when the {enemy_type} found them. I was supposed to go that day, but I stayed behind because I was tired. Tired. That's the reason I'm alive and they're not. {target_npc} has been trying to get me to eat, to sleep, to function. But the only thing that helps is the thought of finishing what we started. I need the materials they were gathering. I need to build that tower.
dialogueChunks.outro: Finishing what they started won't bring them back. But leaving it unfinished feels like losing them twice.

playerResponses.text: Building something in their memory is a way to carry them forward. I'll get the materials.
Survivor's guilt is a heavy burden. I'll help lighten it however I can.
I'm sorry for your loss, but I can't fight your grief for you.
